summary: Africa's most industrialised economy and a moral heavyweight since the end of apartheid, a BRICS member balancing the West, China, and Russia while battling inequality, power cuts, and corruption.

history.detail: British colonies and Boer republics unite under white minority rule.
Police kill 69 protesters; the ANC is banned and turns to armed struggle.
Nelson Mandela released after 27 years; apartheid negotiations begin.
Mandela elected president; ANC begins decades in power.
Hosts the summit inviting new members, signalling Global South leadership.

finance.currency_role: South African rand (ZAR); a widely traded emerging-market currency
leverage_tools: World's largest platinum and manganese reserves
Most developed financial markets in Africa

tech_startups.vc_ecosystem: Mature relative to the continent; fintech and e-commerce

resources.oil_gas: Minimal oil; reliant on coal and imports
resources.nuclear: Koeberg — the only nuclear plant in Africa
resources.rare_earths: World leader in platinum-group metals, gold, manganese, chromium
resources.agriculture: Wine, citrus, maize; a net food exporter

follow_the_money.detail: Platinum, gold and coal remain the backbone of export earnings
The Zondo Commission detailed billions lost to corruption under Zuma
The bankrupt power utility drives chronic blackouts and fiscal risk

theories_disputed.detail: Judicial commission confirmed systematic looting of state firms by the Gupta network.
US alleged weapons were loaded onto a sanctioned Russian ship in 2022; an inquiry found no proof.

connections.detail: Largest trading partner and key BRICS ally
Historic ANC ties; refused to condemn the Ukraine invasion
AGOA trade access alongside political friction over neutrality
